scenario: Tessera Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 over 6500 policies per day and stores nested per-policy data on the process instance as {x:1, y: {z:0}}. A modeller needs the documented call to context put() that updates the nested entry y.z to the value 2 without rewriting the top-level entry x, using the keys-variant of the function on the context built-in functions page.
question: Which call to the keys-variant of context put() updates the nested entry y.z to 2 on {x:1, y: {z:0}}?

options.text: context put({x:1, y: {z:0}}, "y.z", 2); the docs document a dotted-path string as the second argument and state that the engine traverses the dots to update the nested entry y.z on the input context at evaluation

context put({x:1, y: {z:0}}, ["y", "z"], 2); the docs document context put(context, keys: list<string>, value: Any): context and show it returning {x:1, y: {z:2}} when the keys list defines a nested path
context put({x:1, y: {z:0}}, "y", "z", 2); the docs document a variadic form of context put() that accepts each key as its own positional argument and the value as the final positional argument

context put({x:1, y: {z:0}}, {keys: ["y", "z"], value: 2}); the docs document an options-context as the second argument of context put() that bundles the keys list together with the value to assign on the input context

a.text: Incorrect. The keys-variant takes a list of strings rather than a dotted-path string; the docs document context put(context, keys: list<string>, value: Any): context, where the path is a FEEL list, not a string with dots.

b.text: Correct. The context built-in functions page documents context put(context: context, keys: list<string>, value: Any): context. If keys contains [k1, k2], the function adds or overrides the nested entry k1.k2 = value, and the page shows context put({x:1, y: {z:0}}, ["y", "z"], 2) returning {x:1, y: {z:2}}.

c.text: Incorrect. The function does not accept a variadic key list; the docs document the path as a single list-of-strings argument followed by the value argument.

d.text: Incorrect. The function does not accept an options-context; the docs document the keys-variant signature as context put(context, keys: list<string>, value: Any): context with three positional arguments.

explanation: context put(context: context, keys: list<string>, value: Any): context adds a new entry with the given value at the path defined by the keys. If keys contains [k1, k2], it adds or overrides the nested entry k1.k2 = value, and context put({x:1, y: {z:0}}, ["y", "z"], 2) returns {x:1, y: {z:2}}.
